channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports

item.title: Karaban goes to hospital with head injury after No. 2 UConn's loss to Dayton in Maui Invitational
item.description: LAHAINA, Hawaii (AP) — UConn forward Alex Karaban was taken to a hospital with a head injury after the No. 2 Huskies' 85-67 loss to Dayton in the seventh-place game of the Maui Invitational on Wednesday night. Karaban’s head hit the floor after he was fouled with about 2 1/2 minutes left.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Stanford’s struggling offense under microscope ahead of finale at San Jose State
item.description: The Cardinal (3-8) rank 123rd out of 133 FBS teams in yards per game (318.9) and 111th in points (22.1).
item.dc:creator: Harold Gutmann, The Mercury News

item.title: CCS football finals preview: What to know heading into championship games
item.description: Los Gatos, St. Francis both seeking record 17th CCS title. Wilcox, Palma meet for D-II crown. Sacred Heart Prep heats up at right time. Sequoia hopes to go from 0-4 to section champs.
item.dc:creator: Christian Babcock, Darren Sabedra, The Mercury News

item.title: Depleted Jazz lose to Denver, 122-103
item.description: SALT LAKE CITY (ABC4 Sports) - Without injured stars Lauri Markkanen, John Collins and Jordan Clarkson, the Utah Jazz had little chance against the Denver Nuggets and lost for the sixth time in their last seven games.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: New York Jets hire 33rd team to assist in hiring process
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) -- An organizational shift is upon us for the New York Jets. Owner Woody Johnson has hired the 33rd team which was founded by former Jets GM Mike Tannenbaum in 2019, to help aid the Jets search.
item.dc:creator: Marc Malusis, PIX 11

item.title: Caitlin Clark is a bigger star than NBA's best players, famed podcast host says
item.description: Caitlin Clark is the face of women's basketball and women's sports, but podcast host Bill Simmons says she's an even bigger star than the NBA's best.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: West Virginia knocks off No. 3 Gonzaga 86-78 in overtime in the Battle 4 Atlantis
item.description: NASSAU, Bahamas (AP) — Javon Small scored five of his 31 points in overtime and Tucker DeVries added key free throws late in regulation and finished with 16 points as West Virginia beat No. 3 Gonzaga 86-78 in the Battle 4 Atlantis on Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Jason Kelce got 'tons of advice' from longtime late-night host as he embarks on solo act for ESPN
item.description: As he embarks on his latest sports media project with a late-night show, Jason Kelce said he got "tons of advice" from longtime late-night host Jimmy Kimmel.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Raiders will start O'Connell at quarterback when they visit the Chiefs
item.description: HENDERSON, Nev. (AP) — Aidan O'Connell will start at quarterback when the Las Vegas Raiders visit their AFC West rival and two-time defending Super Bowl champion Kansas City Chiefs on Friday.
item.dc:creator: MARK ANDERSON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Blake Snell and Dodgers agree to $182 million, 5-year contract, AP source says
item.description: Less than a month after winning the World Series, the Los Angeles Dodgers are spending big again to add one of baseball's best pitchers to their star-studded roster.
item.dc:creator: RONALD BLUM, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Orlando takes on Chicago, aims for 4th straight win
item.description: Chicago Bulls (8-11, ninth in the Eastern Conference) vs. Orlando Magic (12-7, third in the Eastern Conference) Orlando, Florida; Wednesday, 7 p.m.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: LIV Golf will hire sports and entertainment leader Scott O'Neil to replace Greg Norman, report says
item.description: LIV Golf is hiring Scott O’Neil, former CEO of the Philadelphia 76ers and New Jersey Devils, to replace Greg Norman in running the Saudi-backed rival league about to enter its fourth season, The Athletic reported Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: DOUG FERGUSON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Staley counsels some South Carolina fans to keep calm after No. 4 Gamecocks 1st loss in two seasons
item.description: COLUMBIA, S.C. (AP) — South Carolina coach Dawn Staley is not in panic mode, even if some of her famous “Fams” are following the Gamecocks' first loss in two seasons. Sure, South Carolina has only lost twice in its past 87 games.
item.dc:creator: PETE IACOBELLI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Derrick Henry and Saquon Barkley prepare for earliest matchup ever between 1,300-yard running backs
item.description: Inside the Numbers dives into NFL statistics, streaks and trends each week. For more Inside the Numbers, head here.
item.dc:creator: JOSH DUBOW, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Vucevic and White lead Bulls to 127-108 win over Wizards, losers of 12 straight
item.description: Nikola Vucevic scored 19 points and grabbed 12 rebounds as the Chicago Bulls eased to a 127-108 victory over the struggling Washington Wizards on Tuesday night in an NBA Cup game.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Sergachev scores in overtime in 3-2 Utah HC win
item.description: MONTREAL (ABC4 Sports) - Mikhail Sergachev scored with 27 seconds remaining in overtime to give the Utah Hockey Club a 3-2 win against the Montreal Canadiens at Bell Centre on Tuesday. Sergachev directed a Nick Schmaltz pass through the crease inside the left post.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Jets management turns sights to 2025
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) -- The Jets are a 3 and 8 football team, getting set to host the Seahawks on Sunday. Gone is the head coach. Gone is the general manager. Owner Woody Johnson is running wild.
item.dc:creator: Marc Malusis, PIX 11

item.title: 10 Chicagoland prep football programs will play for an IHSA state championship in Normal this week
item.description: Ten Chicagoland prep football programs will play for an Illinois High School Association state championship later this week in Normal. That includes two based in the city and two that will play each other for the state title in Class 8A, the IHSA's largest division.
item.dc:creator: Michael Johnson, WGN 9

item.title: Column: As offense ascends, Bears defense goes on sneaky decline under Matt Eberflus
item.description: The Chicago Bears' defense has been struggling to stop opposing offenses, resulting in a five-game losing streak, while the offense has been unable to score enough points to win games.
item.dc:creator: Eli Ong, WGN 9

item.title: Giants looked terrible after emotionally draining week
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) -- There is no other way to cut it. The Giants' performance yesterday was putrid. They got embarrassed by the Bucs 30-7. Tommy DeVito got mocked by Tampa quarterback Baker Mayfield. Boo's rained down at MetLife Stadium. Effort was not there.
item.dc:creator: Marc Malusis, PIX 11

item.title: Lightning host Avalanche for 'Hockey Fights Cancer' night
item.description: Although it's just another hockey game on the schedule for the lightning, tonight is bigger than that and means more than just the sport.
item.dc:creator: Aileen Hnatiuk, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Lobos linemen purchase turkeys for families in need with NIL funds
item.description: Some of the players know how hard it can be to put food on the table, so they wanted to extend a hand up.
item.dc:creator: Melissa Torres, NewsNation

item.title: Broncos def. Raiders 29-19
item.description: GRAND JUNCTION, Colo. (KREX)— The Denver Broncos overcame a sluggish start to secure a 29-19 win over the Las Vegas Raiders on Sunday, thanks to a second-half spark from quarterback Bo Nix.
item.dc:creator: Titus Cleveland, KREX 5

item.title: No. 5 UCLA women stun No. 1 South Carolina 77-62, snap Gamecocks' 43-game win streak
item.description: Londynn Jones scored 15 points, making all five of her 3-pointers, and fifth-ranked UCLA stunned No. 1 South Carolina 77-62 on Sunday, ending the Gamecocks’ overall 43-game winning streak and their run of 33 consecutive road victories.
item.dc:creator: BETH HARRIS, Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: Williams unable to carry Bears to victory, Chicago loses in OT to Vikings
item.description: CHICAGO (WGN) — Caleb Williams put on the Superman cape at the end of the fourth quarter, but the Chicago Bears still couldn't get out of their own way in an overtime loss to the Minnesota Vikings on Sunday at Soldier Field. Here's a breakdown of how the game transpired.
item.dc:creator: Eli Ong, WGN 9

item.title: Bears vs. Vikings: What you need to know for Sunday's NFC North showdown at Soldier Field
item.description: CHICAGO — To paraphrase the famous words of then-Indianapolis Colts head coach Jim Mora, the Bears aren't really in a position to think playoffs right now. They're just hoping they can win a game.
item.dc:creator: Michael Johnson, WGN 9

item.title: BYU comeback falls short at ASU, 28-23
item.description: TEMPE, Ariz. (ABC4 Sports) - BYU's offense took too late to get going, and the Cougars' chances at a Big 12 Championship took a serious hit with a 28-23 loss at Arizona State Saturday afternoon.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Michkov scores in overtime, Flyers rally to beat Blackhawks 3-2
item.description: Matvei Michkov scored a power-play goal in overtime as the Flyers overcame a two-goal deficit in the third period to beat the Chicago Blackhawks 3-2 on Saturday.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Giannis gets triple-double as Bucks rout Pacers in NBA Cup
item.description: MILWAUKEE (AP) — Giannis Antetokounmpo had 37 points, 11 assists, and 10 rebounds for his second triple-double in his last four games, and the Milwaukee Bucks beat the Indiana Pacers 129-117 on Friday night.
item.dc:creator: STEVE MEGARGEE, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: No ball at Tropicana Field in 2026 after Milton; new ballpark deal dies: Tampa Bay Rays
item.description: The St. Petersburg City Council has voted to spend over $23 million to repair Tropicana Field after Hurricane Milton destroyed the roof on the home of the Tampa Bay Rays.
item.dc:creator: Ty Russell, WGN 9

item.title: Zach Werenski’s goal in OT and 5-point game helps Blue Jackets to 7-6 win over Lightning
item.description: Zach Werenski’s goal at 1:25 of overtime punctuated a five-point game as the Columbus Blue Jackets rallied from an early three-goal deficit and beat the Tampa Bay Lightning 7-6. Werenski had tied the game at 4 in the second period and added three assists.
item.dc:creator: NICOLE KRAFT, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Corner Canyon wins back-to-back 6A championships
item.description: SALT LAKE CITY (ABC4 Sports) - The Corner Canyon dynasty continues. The Chargers won their second straight 6A state championship, and fourth in the last six years, with a hard-fought 30-27 win over Lone Peak, avenging their lone loss in the regular season.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: No ball at Tropicana Field in 2026; new ballpark deal dies: Rays
item.description: The St. Petersburg City Council has voted to spend over $23 million to repair Tropicana Field after Hurricane Milton destroyed the roof on the home of the Tampa Bay Rays.
item.dc:creator: Garrett Phillips, WFLA, NBC
